Gets the latest version of the specified document. Returns the full document resource including content, styling, and metadata.

query.description: The suggestions view mode to apply to the document. This allows viewing the document with all suggestions inline, collapsed, or only previewing suggestions accepted or rejected. If not specified, defaults to DEFAULT_FOR_CURRENT_ACCESS.

Whether to populate the Document.tabs field instead of the text content fields like body, documentStyle, headers, etc. When true, content is returned in Document.tabs. When false, content from the first tab populates the legacy content fields.

item.name: Google Docs Batch Update a Document

request.name: Google Docs Batch Update a Document

description.content: Applies one or more updates to the document. Each request is validated before being applied. If any request is not valid, the entire request will fail and nothing will be applied. Some requests have replies to give you some information about how they are applied. Other requests do not need to return information; these each return an empty reply. The order of replies matches the order of requests.

response.name: Successful response containing the document ID, ordered replies for each request, and updated write control information.

description.content: Added as a part of security scheme: oauth2

response.name: Bad request. One or more of the update requests were invalid. Nothing was applied to the document.

(Required) The ID of the document to update.

The Google Docs API lets you create, read, and edit Google Docs documents. You can use it to programmatically create and modify documents, apply formatting, insert content, and manage document structure including paragraphs, tables, headers, footers, and inline objects.
